channel.title: The Grant Desk - Remote
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Remote.

item.title: BBQ+ Academic Fellowship 2026-27 - Tiered research stipend by academic level: College students $1,000; Master's/Early PhD $2,000; Advanced PhD $5,000; Recent postdoctoral scholars $10,000. Fellows may also apply to reimburse other research or professionalisation expenses. - Closed 01 May 2026
item.description:
Center for Black, Brown, and Queer Studies (BBQ+), supported by the Mellon Foundation

Location: Remote (fully virtual organisation; all fellowship meetings via Zoom with closed captioning)

Award: Tiered research stipend by academic level: College students $1,000; Master's/Early PhD $2,000; Advanced PhD $5,000; Recent postdoctoral scholars $10,000. Fellows may also apply to reimburse other research or professionalisation expenses.

Duration: Approximately mid-September 2026 through end of April 2027 (~8 months); weekly Friday meetings 12:00 to 14:00 ET

Deadline: Closed 01 May 2026

Annual academic fellowship for underrepresented scholars (broadly and intersectionally defined) working in critical race, Indigenous, postcolonial and queer studies. Open to college students (Sophomores and up, including community college students), recent graduates, Master's students and recent Master's holders (up to 3 years post-degree), PhD/EdD/other doctoral students, and recent postdoctoral scholars (up to 8 years post-degree). Parental, family, sick and similar leaves do not count toward years-post-degree. Open to undocumented and international applicants. Fellows are placed in one of four cohorts (Undergraduate, Master's & Early Doctoral, Advanced Doctoral/Dissertation, Postdoctoral) with a dedicated cohort advisor. Weekly meetings alternate among Writing Clinic, Theory Clinic, Colloquium and Fellows Circle. Application materials: 1-page cover letter, 1-2 page research proposal, CV, and academic writing sample of at least 8 pages of continuous prose. Deadline 1 May 2026 by midnight ET; review begins 4 May 2026; late applications unlikely to receive full consideration. Questions: fellowships@bbqplus.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Rooted Futures Lab: Environmental Justice & Technology (EJIT) Fellowship - Summer 2026 - $3,000 USD stipend (flexible payment schedule) - Closed 30 Apr 2026
item.description:
Rooted Futures Lab

Location: Remote (worldwide)

Award: $3,000 USD stipend (flexible payment schedule)

Duration: ~12 weeks (15 June to 4 September 2026), ~20 hours/week (flexible)

Deadline: Closed 30 Apr 2026

Project-based summer fellowship for individuals working at the intersection of technology, infrastructure and environmental justice. Each Fellow contributes to a defined project area working independently and in close collaboration with Rooted Futures Lab staff and other Fellows. All tracks produce tangible outputs that support communities, advance critical inquiry and strengthen the broader EJIT field. Summer 2026 tracks: (1) Data Centers & Environmental Justice: Creative Technologist Fellow; (2) Data Centers & Environmental Justice: Investigative Storyteller Fellow; (3) Study Hall: Democratic Governance & Collective Systems Fellow; (4) GLITCH Zine: Editor & Strategist Fellow. Applications reviewed on a rolling basis; status notification within 3 weeks of the deadline. Inclusive selection: RFL does not discriminate on sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, race, ethnicity, religion, age or economic background, and explicitly encourages applicants from underrepresented identities. Inspired by Dr. Robert Bullard's environmental justice framework and Octavia Butler's histofuturism. Questions: info@rootedfutureslab.io (subject 'EJIT Fellowship Application').

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Anthropic Fellows Program 2026 (May and July cohorts) - $3,850/week stipend (USD) or £2,310/week (GBP) or $4,300/week (CAD), plus ~$15,000/month compute budget - Rolling / undated
item.description:
Anthropic

Location: Remote (Anthropic-mentored; stipends paid in USD, GBP or CAD)

Award: $3,850/week stipend (USD) or £2,310/week (GBP) or $4,300/week (CAD), plus ~$15,000/month compute budget

Duration: 4 months per cohort (next cohorts start May 2026 and July 2026)

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Funded mentorship program for engineers and researchers to investigate Anthropic's highest-priority AI safety questions: scalable oversight, adversarial robustness and AI control, model organisms, mechanistic interpretability, AI security, and model welfare. Anthropic mentors pitch project ideas; fellows shape and execute them, aiming for public outputs (papers). Over 80% of the first cohort produced papers (on agentic misalignment, subliminal learning, ASL3 jailbreak rapid response, open-source circuits, etc.); over 40% subsequently joined Anthropic full-time. Looking for: strong Python, ability to make concrete progress on ambiguous problems, motivation to reduce catastrophic AI risks. PhD/prior ML experience NOT required; previous fellows came from physics, maths, CS, cybersecurity and other quantitative backgrounds. Two cohorts open: May 2026 and July 2026. The May cohort start is imminent so apply quickly; the July cohort is the more practical near-term option. Apply via the canonical Anthropic Alignment Science page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Kapor Foundation Research Fellowship - Cash award (amount per project; first awards announced by 30 June 2026) - Rolling / undated
item.description:
Kapor Foundation

Location: Remote (US-focused tech policy/research)

Award: Cash award (amount per project; first awards announced by 30 June 2026)

Duration: Per project

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Supports journalists producing long-form investigative reports and tech-policy researchers conducting research, analysis or evaluation that informs policy related to the Kapor Foundation's three priority areas, with an emphasis on responsible AI and tech ethics. Priority areas: CS/AI Education, Innovation, Governance. Aims to dismantle systemic inequities in the tech sector by funding researchers and investigative journalists exploring barriers and driving actionable solutions. Applications accepted on a rolling basis; first awards to be announced by 30 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: O'Shaughnessy Fellowships - Up to $100,000 - Closed 30 Apr 2026
item.description:
OSV Fellowship

Location: Remote

Award: Up to $100,000

Duration: 1 year (remote)

Deadline: Closed 30 Apr 2026

Equity-free fellowship open to adults worldwide. No application fee. Includes access to investors, partners, mentors and optional in-person gatherings for Fellows.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Critical Playground: Freelance Writer Commissions - Paid commissions - Rolling / undated
item.description:
Critical Playground

Location: Remote

Award: Paid commissions

Duration: Per article (800 to 900 words)

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Commissioned long-form writing at the intersection of design, technology, art and culture. Editorial themes: designing with AI as cultural and infrastructural system; responsive and adaptive materials; politics of platforms and creative-infrastructure governance; post-digital hybrid making; designing for collapse and continuity; creative research as practice. In-depth pieces only, no press releases or promotional copy.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Working-Class Creative Grant (Karim Boumjimar) - €500 (no strings attached) - Rolling / undated
item.description:
Karim Boumjimar (independent artist initiative)

Location: Online / Remote

Award: €500 (no strings attached)

Duration: One-time

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Independent artist-run grant redistributing a portion of Karim Boumjimar's artwork sales as a no-strings-attached 500 EUR award for working-class creatives anywhere in the world. Funds may be used for artistic production, research, materials, travel or basic living needs. Simple application: short intro and a sample of work or interests. No fee, no reporting, no obligation to produce. Rolling review based on available funds; non-selected applicants stay in the pool for future rounds.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
